content: We can frame biological pathogenic agents (viruses and bacteria from the chemistry/biology definition of "agent") as naturally evolved autonomous agents that use an innate ICM-like curiosity mechanism to drive their persistent exploration of new host niches. Biological agents gain an implicit "intrinsic reward" from successfully adapting to and colonizing under-explored new host environments, just as 2025 ICM-powered RL swarm UAVs gain internal reward for discovering uncharted target areas.
